title: Father of heav’n, whose love profound
firstLine: Father of heav’n, whose love profound
publicDomainBasis: Edward Cooper (1770–1833), John Bacchus Dykes (1823–1876). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Father of heav’n, whose love profound
A ransom for our souls hath found,
Before Thy throne we sinners bend;
To us Thy pard’ning love extend.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Almighty Son, incarnate Word,
Our Prophet, Priest, Redeemer, Lord,
Before Thy throne we sinners bend;
To us Thy saving grace extend.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Eternal Spirit, by whose breath
The soul is raised from sin and death,
Before Thy throne we sinners bend;
To us Thy quickening power extend.

sections.label: Verse 4
